name: Tom Cotton
subject: POLL: Stop Chinese Espionage on Campus?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
body:
The Chinese government uses a program called the “Thousand Talents Plan” to steal from our universities to benefit the Chinese Communist Party. Recently, the chair of Harvard University’s Chemistry Department was arrested for receiving money from the Chinese Communist Party and lying to the Department of Defense.Click here to unsubscribe http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/servlet/OneClickOptOutServlet?m=42365984&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MjgzNjQwOAS2&mt=1&rt=0 Peter,

Your tax dollars go to support public colleges and universities.

But did you know that the Chinese Communist Party is stealing intellectual property from those same colleges and sending it back to China?

The Chinese government uses a program called the “Thousand Talents Plan” to steal from our universities to benefit the Chinese Communist Party. Recently, the chair of Harvard University’s Chemistry Department was arrested for receiving money from the Chinese Communist Party and lying to the Department of Defense.

The bottom line is that China’s infiltration of our campuses is a major problem.
That’s why I want to restrict visas for Chinese nationals who may try to steal important property from America—including a potential vaccine for the China virus.

I think it’s common sense. We should protect our own interests, not care about the hurt feelings of the murderous Chinese Communist Party.

But now, I’m under attack from the media and liberals who are soft on China.
Instead of holding the murderous Chinese regime accountable for their role in the spread of this virus, these liberals are attacking me for speaking the truth.

Peter, the media is launching an attack and I want to know if you agree with me:

Should we ban student visas for Chinese nationals working with the Chinese Communist Party to threaten our national security? http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=12&ms=NDIzNjU5ODQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MjgzNjQwOAS2&mt=1&rt=0
YES http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=NDIzNjU5ODQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MjgzNjQwOAS2&mt=1&rt=0 NO http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=14&ms=NDIzNjU5ODQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MjgzNjQwOAS2&mt=1&rt=0
Peter, I hope you will continue to stand with me as we expose the truth about the China Virus.

I want to know what you think, so please take my quick poll.
Take the Poll http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=NDIzNjU5ODQS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0MjgzNjQwOAS2&mt=1&rt=0

In service,

Tom

Suite 200
Little Rock, AR 72202

Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Kathy=20Tall?=
subject: Your weekly update!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dani for Illinois
body:
Hello #TeamDani! We've made some great strides this last week and have a lot of exciting upcoming opportunities! | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/4903124f8f3a/your-weekly-update?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#

**Hello, fabulous Dani for Illinois volunteers!** **From all of us here at #TeamDani, I hope you had a great weekend and are staying healthy. If you are in need of any community resources or support during this crisis, do not hesitate to reach out.** **Thank you all SO MUCH for your incredible work this week and for your support of the campaign. As we do every week, check out some highlights and reminders below:**
* **We completed calls to over 2,600 voters in the 16th District!**
* **Our biggest day was last Tuesday, with 620 calls just that day! **
* **Huge shoutout to Mary and Hannah, our top volunteer and intern this week, for completing over 700 dials combined!**
* **We have sent out 1,020 postcards to volunteer writers all throughout the District to engage voters and, of course, #SaveUSPS!**
* **Over 700 people tuned in to our Healthcare Town Hall last Monday!**

**A few reminders:**

* **If you have friends/colleagues/family members/people you bump into at the grocery store (from 6 feet away, of course) who may be interested in volunteering, please have them reach out or send me their contact info!**

* **On May 4th, we will be hosting our[first digital fundraiser and engagement event with Illinois NOW and Rust Belt Rising!](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=cd122b07c3&e=140a9b81e7)**

* **We will be hosting a conference call for all of you this Wednesday at noon! Please join us, meet each other, and get updates from the team. Dial in #[253-993-3246](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=56871ef07c&e=140a9b81e7).**

**Again, from all of us on #TeamDani, thank you! Keep up the good work, let’s flip this seat together.** **Kathy Tall** **Deputy Field Director**
---
| | | | | | | [](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=94429a548e&e=140a9b81e7)
---
| | [](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=3b9776d473&e=140a9b81e7)

---

Dani for Illinois

Lasalle, IL 61301

You can [update your preferences](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/profile?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=7369c912a5&e=140a9b81e7) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=7369c912a5&e=140a9b81e7&c=2216bbf5e9).

name: Mike Franken
subject: Just one month
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Franken for Iowa
body:
In race parlance, we are doing the four-minute mile -- a 40 day sprint to the primary. Next week will be the 1000-meter run as we coordinate last-minute endorsements and ensure we reach out to all mai
|
---
| |
---

| | Hello everyone, and thank you for giving me a minute. In race parlance, we are doing the four-minute mile -- a 40-day sprint to the primary. Next week will be the 1000-meter run as we coordinate last-minute endorsements and ensure we reach out to all mail-in voters. Other race parallelisms will follow as the team prepares a first-place finish on June 2. I hope you have seen our ad on television and social media. The overarching message is two-fold: I can beat Joni Ernst, and I can help lead the recovery, the rebuilding, and the renewal of America. This is not a time for training wheels. Experience counts, and that's something I'd like to use for all Iowans. **The next seven days will be crucial. We have the right message, but we need the necessary funds to let everyone in Iowa know that they have a unique candidate to win the general election in November.** [_**We 're looking to raise $100,000 by May 2nd -- exactly one month before primary day. Can you chip in $5, $10, or $20 now? **_](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f24a7/33365068/4a74603d/3f8bf477/4291778287/VEsH/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mf2020q2?refcode=FRA_20200427_eom1_em_fr_all_l1") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5 >>](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f24a7/33365068/4a74603d/3f8bf470/4291778287/VEsE/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mf_ad?amount=5&express_lane=true&refcode=FRA_20200427_eom1_em_fr_all_abx5")
---

---

Thank you, and see you in the winner's circle,
Mike Franken
Vice Admiral, USN ret.
Sioux City, IA

Hi Peter,

Our April deadline is coming up -- but with everything going on, it's looking like **_we won 't reach our goal_**.

We need a one-day push to get there. **[Will you pitch in now?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489031a/7f51f7bc/4f356caf/4ef17293/77613547/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEyODYifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200427.2nd__1286__&amount=10")**

MJ is relying on grassroots donors, not Big Pharma and corporate PACs. But with your support, we'll keep expanding our team, running digital outreach to voters across the state, and building the Texas-sized campaign we need to elect a Senate that works for all Texans.

**Peter, we can 't do this without you.

Can we count on you to rush a donation at this link to help us reach our goal? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/MJ_April_URGENT](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489031a/7f51f7bc/4f356caf/4ef17293/77613547/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEyODYifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200427.2nd__1286__&amount=10")**

Thanks for all that you do,

\-- MJ Hegar Senior Staff

**[**DONATE**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489031a/7f51f7bc/4f356caf/4ef17293/77613547/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEyODYifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200427.2nd__1286__&amount=10")**

|

| It's clear we need a real leader for Texas and a Senate that prioritizes our health care. **[More than ever, we're relying on online fundraising to keep building the campaign we need to win in November, and we greatly appreciate your continued support>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489031a/7f51f7bc/4f356caf/4ef17290/77613547/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEyODYifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-covidfooter?refcode=1286&amount=10")**

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Here's the truth about vote-by-mail:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
body:
This petition is incredibly important right now.
| **From the Desk of
Jay Inslee** Friend, I want to be crystal clear: Here in Washington state, vote-by-mail has been a spectacular success. So when President Trump says that vote-by-mail is more susceptible to voter fraud than other forms of voting, he's wrong. Plain and simple. Washington state has proven that vote-by-mail is the easiest, safest, most reliable voting there is. This isn't a partisan issue -- in both Republican- and Democrat-run states, vote-by-mail has _increased_ voter participation. There is zero reason **not** to have a mail-in ballot in any condition and certainly not when people have to risk their lives to go physically vote right now during this COVID-19 pandemic. **Now, I'm calling for vote-by-mail to be a right for every American across this country.[Join me in calling for national vote-by-mail: Add your name right now.](https://act.jayinslee.com/sign/support-vbm_copy/?utm_source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&utm_campaign=supportvbm&utm_medium=ema&t=3&akid=8051%2E868730%2EqEoHD_)** | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.jayinslee.com/sign/support-vbm_copy/?utm_source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&utm_campaign=supportvbm&utm_medium=ema&t=4&akid=8051%2E868730%2EqEoHD_)
---
The photos of mile-long lines -- people in masks forced to either risk their health or give up their right to vote -- should break the heart of every American, regardless of whether they're a Democrat or Republican. And we must not let this happen in November.
You'll never see a line out the door to vote in Washington state. You will never wait in the rain, in the cold, or in the heat to fill in your ballot. Your ballot box should be as close as your mailbox. In fact, we even take care of the postage.
We already knew that too many Americans were choosing between voting and making it to their jobs, or voting and childcare, but now, all Americans are choosing between their health and their vote. That's unacceptable.
All of President Trump's claims -- that vote-by-mail is "corrupt" and "ripe for fraud" and never allows Republicans to be elected again -- are not based in reality. Plus, the president actually votes absentee by mail -- so if it's good enough for him, why not anyone else?
Here in Washington, our own secretary of state says that our system is "accessible, secure, fair, and instills confidence in our voters."
This isn't about politics. It's about commonsense solutions to help people vote safely and more easily this year and in the years to come. [**If you believe vote-by-mail will work for all Americans just like it's worked for Washingtonians, add your name next to mine right now.**](https://act.jayinslee.com/sign/support-vbm_copy/?utm_source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&utm_campaign=supportvbm&utm_medium=ema&t=5&akid=8051%2E868730%2EqEoHD_)
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.jayinslee.com/sign/support-vbm_copy/?utm_source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&source=ema_2020-04-24_supportvbm&utm_campaign=supportvbm&utm_medium=ema&t=6&akid=8051%2E868730%2EqEoHD_)
---

PO BOX 21067 SEATTLE, WA 98111

Theresa is the **best candidate** to take on her Republican opponent, Senator Ernst. She understands the problems everyday Americans are facing because she's faced them too. name: Team Warren
subject: A message for you about our important April fundraising goal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren Democrats
body:
Our country is going through a difficult time right now -- and Elizabeth has been busy. In the past month, she's helped lead the charge for an Essential Workers Bill of Rights, fought to put people... | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257d6/45c30650/1f5435d01/66e91e0b/3459297005/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Our country is going through a difficult time right now -- and Elizabeth has been busy.

In the past month, she's helped lead the charge for an Essential Workers Bill of Rights, fought to put people first as we get through this crisis, shined a light on the Trump administration's failures of leadership, and endorsed 20 terrific Democratic candidates up and down the ballot.

She's still in this fight, but she can't do it alone. As Warren Democrats, we need to fight by her side.

Together, we can fill our government with more leaders who will fight for our progressive values. We can keep the voices of working people front and center as we recover from this crisis and rebuild our country. And we can make bold, inclusive reform to address the root causes of how we got into this mess.

**We only have three more days left to hit our April fundraising goal and fully fund our outreach for May. We understand that with everything else happening, not everyone is going to be able to make a financial contribution to this movement right now.

[But if you're in a place where you're able to donate, a contribution of $2 or anything you can would go a long way -- to keep growing our movement, to elect Democrats like Elizabeth, and to fight for big, structural change.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257d6/45c30650/1f5435d01/66e91e0d/3459297005/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LXdhcnJlbmRlbW9jcmF0cy1hcHIyMGZyIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVEyM0ZfRU9NX0xBVV9UXzIwMjAwNDI3X1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-warrendemocrats-apr20fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMQ23F_EOM_LAU_T_20200427_V1S3_P")**

2020 is the most important election of our lifetimes, and we need all hands on deck.

This year, we need to send Donald Trump packing, keep the House, take back the Senate, and elect state legislators and governors who'll keep Republicans from gerrymandering themselves into power after next year's redistricting.

The fight won't be over in November -- we'll need to keep working to end corruption and make our government work for everyone -- but we also can't wait until November.

So we'll keep advocating day in and day out for the pandemic relief measures we desperately need today, and we'll make sure we elect Democrats up and down the ballot to see this crisis through for working families. Side by side, we'll root out corruption in government and put power in the hands of the people.

**We 're building a powerful organizing network to get this done -- but it takes real resources. It's going to take a grassroots movement funded by grassroots donors like you.

clean_body: Our country is going through a difficult time right now -- and Elizabeth has been busy. In the past month, she's helped lead the charge for an Essential Workers Bill of Rights, fought to put people... | | | "" Our country is going through a difficult time right now -- and Elizabeth has been busy. In the past month, she's helped lead the charge for an Essential Workers Bill of Rights, fought to put people first as we get through this crisis, shined a light on the Trump administration's failures of leadership, and endorsed 20 terrific Democratic candidates up and down the ballot. She's still in this fight, but she can't do it alone. As Warren Democrats, we need to fight by her side. Together, we can fill our government with more leaders who will fight for our progressive values. We can keep the voices of working people front and center as we recover from this crisis and rebuild our country. And we can make bold, inclusive reform to address the root causes of how we got into this mess. We only have three more days left to hit our April fundraising goal and fully fund our outreach for May. We understand that with everything else happening, not everyone is going to be able to make a financial contribution to this movement right now. "" 2020 is the most important election of our lifetimes, and we need all hands on deck. This year, we need to send Donald Trump packing, keep the House, take back the Senate, and elect state legislators and governors who'll keep Republicans from gerrymandering themselves into power after next year's redistricting. The fight won't be over in November -- we'll need to keep working to end corruption and make our government work for everyone -- but we also can't wait until November. So we'll keep advocating day in and day out for the pandemic relief measures we desperately need today, and we'll make sure we elect Democrats up and down the ballot to see this crisis through for working families. Side by side, we'll root out corruption in government and put power in the hands of the people. We 're building a powerful organizing network to get this done -- but it takes real resources. It's going to take a grassroots movement funded by grassroots donors like you. "" Thanks for everything, and stay safe, Warren Democrats | "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren Democrats All content (C) 2020 Warren Democrats, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

subject: An update on San Diego’s COVID-19 response:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Georgette Gomez for Congress
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17285325/189299156/1001356148?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWJhYzk5MS1kNDg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M2Hn81ICs3XAhpUUiI_g-D1EstKgnJ8ZBKQS6DAddMU=&emci=c9f62b3d-c988-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=e9bac991-d488-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=8780864)
Friend --

I wanted to take a moment and share the latest updates regarding our city’s response to COVID-19.

_**But I want to start off by saying thank you.**_

From the beginning, the city council and our mayor came to the shared conclusion that if our response was going to succeed, we’d all have to work together. That includes with other cities in our region, county officials, the state, and of course, _residents like you._

We’ve followed every recommendation and implemented some unique changes of our own to keep each other safe and do our part to flatten the curve. And with your support it’s working.

As the weather heats up, it's only natural that we want to head for the San Diego beaches, parks, and trails that we all know and love.

The good news is that we were able to do a soft opening of some city parks to provide an outlet for people to be physically and mentally active. But it’s critical that we maintain our social distance — even at parks. We’re hopeful that residents will continue to take the measures seriously so this soft opening can continue.

Here’s some of what I’m keeping an eye on this week: **\- Limited Reopening of Beaches:** Today, San Diego's beaches are open to surfers, swimmers, and runners on the sand. _Please note that group gatherings and lying down on the beach are not allowed._

**\- CARES Act Funding:** Last week, I sent a letter to Mayor Faulconer urging him to maximize the $248 million in federal CARES Act funding that we’ve been allocated. As City Council President, it’s my goal to make sure our most vulnerable are not left behind.

**\- Otay Mesa Detention Center:** I’ve called on Gavin Newsom to join me in calling for a _drastic reduction_ in the number of people held at the Otay Mesa Detention Center where a COVID-19 outbreak has infected detainees and staff. Detaining people in these unsafe circumstances is cruel and must end. **[You can add your name to the petition to join this effort here >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17285326/189299157/1007659402?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWJhYzk5MS1kNDg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M2Hn81ICs3XAhpUUiI_g-D1EstKgnJ8ZBKQS6DAddMU=&emci=c9f62b3d-c988-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=e9bac991-d488-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=8780864) ** I’ll continue to keep you up to date. In the meantime, stay safe — and as much as you can, stay inside.

_Georgette Gómez_
_We understand this is a difficult time, so please take care of yourself and your loved ones. We have a crucial end-of-month fundraising deadline coming up, and need some support. But please only chip in to my congressional campaign if you can afford to do so right now. _ **[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gomez-q2-2020?refcode=em-04272020-all-a)**

---
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17285327/189299158/1001356148?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWJhYzk5MS1kNDg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M2Hn81ICs3XAhpUUiI_g-D1EstKgnJ8ZBKQS6DAddMU=&emci=c9f62b3d-c988-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&emdi=e9bac991-d488-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&ceid=8780864) Georgette Gómez is the first LGBTQ Latina elected San Diego City Council President. Now, she's running for Congress to stand up to Trump’s dangerous agenda and recenter our focus on working families in our community.

_**Georgette is the only candidate in the race for CA-53 to be endorsed by the California Democratic Party.**_ Email is the best way to stay up to date with Georgette's campaign. To support Georgette's campaign please [make a donation here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17285328/189299159/1147193815?refcode=em-footer&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWJhYzk5MS1kNDg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M2Hn81ICs3XAhpUUiI_g-D1EstKgnJ8ZBKQS6DAddMU=). We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to [unsubscribe](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/c9f62b3d-c988-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd/e9bac991-d488-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWJhYzk5MS1kNDg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M2Hn81ICs3XAhpUUiI_g-D1EstKgnJ8ZBKQS6DAddMU=&id=110960909&emailAddress=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). Thank you for supporting Georgette! Paid for by Georgette Gomez for Congress

name: Laura Loomer
subject: "Plastic Face Heeb"
body:
When I announced my run for Congress, a spokesman for the Democrat Congressional Campaign Committee told the media, “A full-blown white nationalist is running for Congress in Florida. Republicans must condemn Loomer’s hate-filled message or they own it.”
| | | [](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-373632-878672-4973906-e68cb80999-e44755de75)
Patriot,

[](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-373632-878672-4973907-cfbdc9d66d-e44755de75) When I announced my run for Congress, a spokesman for the Democrat Congressional Campaign Committee told the media, **“A full-blown white nationalist is running for Congress in Florida. Republicans must condemn Loomer’s hate-filled message or they own it.”**

Right out of the gate, just days after my announcement, the Democrats attempted to label me as a racist and define me as a White Supremacist despite the reality that I’m a conservative Jewish woman.

So while the Democrats try to define me as a racist, ACTUAL racists write articles with headlines like this about me: **“Plastic Face Heeb Laura Loomer Gets Donations from Rich Jews for Congressional Campaign”**.

They can’t have it both ways – but that’s politics and they’ll try anything to stop people like you from supporting me.

**If you’re able,[show them you don’t believe their lies by donating today](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-373632-878672-4973907-cfbdc9d66d-e44755de75).**

They’ll do anything to keep me from winning this election in President Trump’s backyard.

But they’re losing!

Since I announced my campaign for Congress, we have not only outraised the Democrat incumbent – we’ve done it THREE TIMES IN A ROW!

[**Help me make it a fourth time by donating today.**](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-373632-878672-4973907-cfbdc9d66d-e44755de75)

We’re about to close out the first month of the quarter and we need to raise another $32,000 by Friday!

[Any amount that you can donate makes a dent in that number whether it’s $100 or $5, every dollar is appreciated.](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-373632-878672-4973907-cfbdc9d66d-e44755de75)

While a true racist from an Alt-Right publication attacked me, calling me an “Israel First hook-noser,” I have to say this:

**When they attack me, THEY ATTACK ALL OF US.**

**There’s no better way to prove to them that each time they write a piece like this, all they do is motivate you to support my campaign.**

[Show your support now, help me close out April as strong as possible by donating all that you can today](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-373632-878672-4973907-cfbdc9d66d-e44755de75)

Thank you for all that you do.

Respectfully,

Laura Loomer

body:
.
It's no secret that the Democrats have a huge digital fundraising advantage over us here in Illinois so we are asking for patriots like you to help us catch up over this critical end of month period.
Friend,
April is the first full month that our campaign has been entirely digital and unfortunately, it's how our campaign has to operate going forward for the foreseeable future.
It's no secret that the Democrats have a huge digital fundraising advantage over us here in Illinois so we are asking for patriots like you to help us catch up over this critical end of month period.
If we were to close the book on April today, we'd still be [**$3,235** ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001rd1BOvpzODf9ctr-o59FG-vxsoZ9CctlNPUFTh1ECi_ciBiYVQ4u9ymaaYNnyHK-slRx40--X55X2HqqDu_J5NAT7YtkSFljEvlGCzSbV0sIOfN5-y-v1q85FAenEmtADIM_kEb_wNprY9nzGcJ5Fa9uXJkTsds-f_s0ntnSrjpfl6SFRu3EL7w2rFhLp_RWCDYZqRUn4KBRlL4MX4eOwrrk5mZ2viAFSp4V6qAYSyDsjrDEkigqRmzMOgN4LqHV4fiuH_dWfUFjg5Vxq0NaZblJNEGQ8ggMnCf5M1enBz4_M7Z4aXMKD1tEO0u7hQ7zHLH6PV7t8uweN96vX6M_3E93Mc1Xi-9FMFSwodlWQXdwWH1YfjCX6-gE7FbX1ByLOMibcDc8cRyw_bwOWzB_K-yGuZ0qxVq8&c=JFUf_oSkz-JjgId9gxUburp415lu_REegvb1CnbdNGOcEvab3oxXkw==&ch=zaQl3-gTnDEaYDS6wN6ZSy5IR-NTwMXSvVb1xjYlEALe6R9-4_vrjw==)short of our goal. name: Colin Lauderdale
subject: You and 499 others
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
We're calling on our movement to help us reach 500 donors by the end of the month. Can you help?
#
We're in the final week of April, which means we got a fundraising deadline – and a goal to meet – this week.
But rather than going for a dollar-amount goal this month, we're setting a new goal: **we're calling on our movement to help us reach 500 donors by the end of the month.**
No specific dollar amount. No high dollar-based goal. We just want to find 500 people who are hungry like we are to defeat Mitch McConnell once and for all.
**If you already understand how important this is, then click below to chip in and become one of the 500 we need:**
****[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17285484/189303197/-1771280061?refcode=em20200427&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMmE0MjlmMy1kNTg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eLH7YgSSyYw5RMPzRf4Zr3Pk4gbb0YUmQVZdRPObNEk=)****
Our people-centered, progressive grassroots movement – and its dedicated supporters like yourself – are what makes our campaign truly special.
We believe in fighting for bold, progressive change.
We answer the call for help where it's needed.
And we believe that the solution to beat Mitch McConnell isn't some middle-of-the-road Democrat – it's all of us, working together.
If we can reach 500 donors by the end of April, it'll be a strong first step in showing political pundits and the media that while our campaign isn't the establishment favorite or as wealthy as the others, we have the strongest base. **The only way we're going to defeat Mitch is if we build a people-powered movement that's ready to fight this November.**
If we can reach 500 donors by the end of April, it'll be a strong first step in showing political pundits and the media that while our campaign isn't the establishment favorite or as wealthy as the others, we have the strongest base. **The only way we're going to defeat Mitch is if we build a people-powered movement that's ready to fight this November.**
****[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17285498/189303211/-1771280061?refcode=em20200427&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMmE0MjlmMy1kNTg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eLH7YgSSyYw5RMPzRf4Zr3Pk4gbb0YUmQVZdRPObNEk=)****
Not setting a dollar-based goal for a political campaign, especially in the final months, isn't typical. But we're not a typical campaign.
Charles has always said that this Senate campaign is about all of us. Not about him, not about the Democratic establishment, and certainly not about Mitch McConnell. It's about every day, hardworking people, from Kentucky to the rest of our nation.
More soon,
Colin Lauderdale, Campaign Manager
Booker for Kentucky

** **

****[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17285499/189303212/-1771280061?refcode=em20200427-footer&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMmE0MjlmMy1kNTg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eLH7YgSSyYw5RMPzRf4Zr3Pk4gbb0YUmQVZdRPObNEk=)****

We believe in fighting for bold, progressive change. We answer the call for help where it's needed. And we believe that the solution to beat Mitch McConnell isn't some middle-of-the-road Democrat – it's all of us, working together. If we can reach 500 donors by the end of April, it'll be a strong first step in showing political pundits and the media that while our campaign isn't the establishment favorite or as wealthy as the others, we have the strongest base. The only way we're going to defeat Mitch is if we build a people-powered movement that's ready to fight this November. If we can reach 500 donors by the end of April, it'll be a strong first step in showing political pundits and the media that while our campaign isn't the establishment favorite or as wealthy as the others, we have the strongest base. The only way we're going to defeat Mitch is if we build a people-powered movement that's ready to fight this November. = Not setting a dollar-based goal for a political campaign, especially in the final months, isn't typical. But we're not a typical campaign. Charles has always said that this Senate campaign is about all of us. Not about him, not about the Democratic establishment, and certainly not about Mitch McConnell. It's about every day, hardworking people, from Kentucky to the rest of our nation. More soon, Colin Lauderdale, Campaign Manager Booker for Kentucky =** | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Booker for Kentucky

Earlier this month, we told you about the massive investments by special interest groups trying to influence our race. The largest Republican super PAC in the country is investing **$43 million** into flipping the House red, and propping up Scott Perry is one of their top priorities. During this difficult time, please only consider contributing to our campaign if you’re able to. Team, We just found out that Scott Perry outraised us in the first quarter of 2020 -- by almost $100,000. Now, we’re facing our April fundraising deadline, and we need to raise another $10,000 by this Thursday so that we can invest in the resources we need for this ultra competitive campaign. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = = Earlier this month, we told you about the massive investments by special interest groups trying to influence our race. The largest Republican super PAC in the country is investing $43 million into flipping the House red, and propping up Scott Perry is one of their top priorities. The far-right special interest group Club for Growth is pouring over $500,000 into this race. And, a pro-Trump super PAC is investing $10 million into battleground states, including Pennsylvania. If we fall short of our April fundraising goal, it could put us in a position where Scott Perry outraises us again. And, if we want to be able to compete against the big, special-interest money pouring into our district,we absolutely cannot let that happen, team. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = = Thank you, Team Eugene Follow Eugene on social media for campaign updates: Eugene DePasquale is running to represent Pennsylvania's 10th Congressional District. name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: More help on the way, but more to do
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501143683%2501100444%2b-%2b04-14-2020%2beNewsletter%250115407.16260280.4540656.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501143683%2501100444%2b-%2b04-14-2020%2beNewsletter%250115407.16260280.4540656.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **April 27, 2020** |
---|---|---|---
| | | I hope that you and your family are staying safe and healthy. As we fight this virus, I want to update you on what we're doing as Team Maryland to get through this together and provide information on how to access assistance if you need it.

The good news: Marylanders are coming together while apart -- I hear inspiring stories every day about neighbors looking out for each other, and everyone rallying to support our frontline healthcare workers. The hard news: parts of our state have become COVID-19 hotspots. To handle the surge and look toward reopening our state, we need increased testing capacity, medical supplies, and protective equipment -- and continued assistance to unemployed workers, small businesses, and non-profit organizations so people can stay home and avoid transmitting the disease.

To understand and meet the needs of communities across our state, I've been holding daily virtual meetings with leaders and communities across Maryland -- health care and education officials, county and municipal governments, faith leaders, frontline workers, seniors, and non-profit and business groups. These discussions convince me that together, Marylanders will get through this crisis. Congress passed "Coronavirus Phase 3.5" legislation last week to address several urgent priorities, and we're continuing to fight for others. We're pushing the Trump Administration to create a national testing strategy and fully utilize the Defense Production Act to increase the availability of necessary equipment. Here are specific steps we're taking as Team Maryland to help Marylanders, including additional measure in the bill passed last week: Many of you have experienced extreme frustration as you've tried to access the Economic Injury Disaster Loans and Grants (EIDL) and the Paycheck Protection Program (PPP). I worked to address some of those concerns in the Senate bill, and am continuing to push the Treasury Department to cut red tape to help the business that are most impacted by shut-down orders. **Paycheck Protection Program** : The Phase 3.5 bill increases PPP funding by $310 billion, and the program resumed accepting applications today. I encourage anyone who needs this assistance to apply quickly. To prevent tipping the scales in favor of big companies, we set aside $60 billion in the new bill to be lent through smaller financial institutions, such as credit unions, community banks, minority depository institutions, and community development financial institutions. This provision is designed to help mom-and-pop businesses, minority- and women-owned businesses, and small farms that might not have a direct line to a commercial bank. **Economic Injury Disaster Loans** : We provided support for an additional $350 billion in EIDL loans and made sure that small farms are eligible. I encourage anyone who needs this assistance to apply quickly. **Emergency Economic Injury****Grants** : We secured an additional $10 billion for these grants in the EIDL program. You can get the $10,000 grant by applying for an EIDL Loan. It does not have to be repaid, even if you do not meet the conditions to ultimately receive an EIDL loan. **Work sharing:** Maryland has an innovative work sharing program, and we secured federal funding to cover the full cost of expanding it. I encourage employers facing layoffs to consider this program. Find [more information here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.dllr.state.md.us%2femployment%2fworksharing%2f&redir_log=403158732602370). **Remaining challenges: **Despite the progress made in Phase 3.5, we still need to fix problems that are preventing some small businesses, including many restaurants, from making effective use of PPP funds. For example, the 60-day forgivable loan period is not aligned with the time many businesses can expect to see customers return, and the Treasury Department rules put unreasonable limits on the share of PPP funds that can be used for fixed expenses like rent. I will continue fighting to improve these critical programs. **For more information:** See my [Small and Medium Business & Non-profit Guide to the CARES Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fimo%2fmedia%2fdoc%2fVan%2520Hollen+Small%2c+Medium+Business+Owner%2c+Non-profit+Guide+to+CARES+Act+as+of+4-3%255b5%255d.pdf&redir_log=212087952736904) for information on these programs and others. **Unemployment insurance (UI)** : I share the frustration of the many Marylanders who are having difficulty accessing unemployment benefits and the state's new application website, which launched Friday. My team and I have been in touch with the Maryland Department of Labor repeatedly, and we will keep monitoring and offering all possible assistance until these issues are fixed.

Congress previously expanded UI to more workers, increased the amount of unemployment payments workers can receive by $600 per week, extended benefits by 13 weeks, and provided funds to help states process applications and get checks out faster. **Your benefits, including the additional $600/week, will be backdated and paid based on your original eligibility date** , so you will ultimately receive the help you urgently need. However, the backlog and delays are causing severe cash flow problems for many households. **Unemployment insurance applicants, please note:**
* If you are newly eligible for unemployment benefits in Maryland, such as independent contractors, I encourage you to apply at the State of Maryland's new unemployment website, [MDunemployment.com](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=http%3a%2f%2fmdunemployment.com%2f&redir_log=074309390446185). Select "Apply for Unemployment Insurance Benefits" in the left hand column. This will take you to the BEACON one-stop application page.

* The State's new, one-stop unemployment insurance application is designed to allow Marylanders to file claims for all unemployment programs entirely online. This includes the Pandemic Unemployment Assistance program, the Pandemic Emergency Unemployment Compensation program, and those who were previously required to file by phone like federal employees, members of the military, individuals who have worked in multiple states, and those who have worked for more than 3 employers in the last 18 months.

* If you have questions for the unemployment office, try reviewing [their FAQ page](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.dllr.state.md.us%2femployment%2fuicaresfaqs.pdf&redir_log=318923640531391) before calling, because the phone hold times are very long.

We continue to prioritize stopping the spread of the virus and ensuring that frontline healthcare providers are equipped to respond. The most recent legislation (Phase 3.5) adds an additional $75 billion to the $100 billion fund for hospitals, community health centers, senior care facilities and other health care providers. Importantly, this legislation also includes $25 billion to help build out a rapid national COVID-19 testing capability. Scientists and public health leaders agree: we can only get the virus under control, manage future outbreaks, and safely reopen our economy with a robust national strategy that expands access to COVID-19 testing. The next bill must build on this down payment to dramatically expand the national production and distribution of all the elements needed for a rapid, national testing capability, including a sufficient supply of swabs, transport kits, and reagents. While the additional relief in Phase 3.5 is urgently needed, the bill left out several important priorities.

Many families need increased food aid, but Republicans in the Senate blocked additional relief. In addition, our state and local governments, and the emergency responders on the front lines of the battle against COVID-19, are experiencing severe revenue losses and need more help.

While the CARES Act provided an important $150 billion down payment to support state and local governments, more is needed. And there are counterproductive restrictions on the use of the funds. I was appalled to hear Senator McConnell recklessly suggest that states and municipalities could just declare bankruptcy - we should be helping them support first responders, firefighters, and the many daily services our local governments provide. Providing additional and more flexible assistance to state and local governments must be a priority in the next relief bill.

We need to end the digital divide that further deepens inequality in our schools. Too many of our students haven't had access to the internet at home to do homework -- and now to do their daily schoolwork. I was pleased to help lead the effort in the CARES Act to ensure that emergency funds for our schools can be used to provide students with internet access and devices. But the next round of legislation needs a larger pool of funds dedicated exclusively to building out high-speed internet access to every household. Like electricity in the 20th century, we should have universal access to high speed internet in the twenty-first.

Americans overwhelmingly want our country to come together to solve these problems. We need to start work on the next major coronavirus relief bill immediately. There is so much more to do to ensure that the recovery, when it comes, lifts everyone - especially those too often left behind in the past.

There are tough days ahead, but I believe the determination and generosity of spirit of our fellow Marylanders will see us through this crisis to better days. Chris Van Hollen To learn more about Senator Van Hollen's work to address the COVID-19 crisis, visit vanhollen.senate.gov/coronavirus. During the coronavirus crisis, many federal funds have been distributed to Maryland based on our population. It's a reminder of the importance of getting yourself counted! If you have not already done so, please head to [my2020census.gov](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmy2020census.gov%2f&redir_log=574467404893169) to fill out the questionnaire right away, or do it by phone or mail. It takes less than ten minutes for most people.
---
## Protections and Resources for Marylanders
In case you missed our last newsletter, here are some helpful resources. And you can always reach out to us if you need help with federal agencies or programs. You may call my office at 301-545-1500 or email [assistance@vanhollen.senate.gov](mailto:assistance@vanhollen.senate.gov).
* **No evictions:** The state has ordered that no Maryland family or business who suffered a substantial loss of income due to COVID-19 or related closures can be evicted during the coronavirus state of emergency. The obligation to make rent and mortgage payments remains. Landlords may continue to file failure to pay rent cases, which will be considered after the state of emergency ends.

* **No repossessions:** Marylanders' cars, mobile homes, trailers, and house boats cannot be repossessed during the coronavirus emergency. The obligation to make rent and mortgage payments remains. Landlords may continue to file failure to pay rent cases, which will be considered after the state of emergency ends.

* **Senior citizens** seeking information and resources on COVID-19 can [click here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2faging.maryland.gov%2fPages%2fNovel-coronavirus.aspx&redir_log=385359970431391).

* To **find school meals** nearby, visit the Maryland State Department of Education's [meals site](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmars.msde.maryland.gov%2fmars_sitesearch%2f&redir_log=648767416991333).

* To **find child care** , call Maryland's LOCATE referral system at 1-877-261-0060, or [click here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=http%3a%2f%2fwww.marylandfamilynetwork.org%2fprograms-services%2flocate%2f&redir_log=074088812453442).

* For **help paying utility bills** , visit [Maryland's Office of Home Energy Programs](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=http%3a%2f%2fdhr.maryland.gov%2foffice-of-home-energy-programs%2fhow-do-you-apply%2f&redir_log=584818711733502).

* For **temporary cash assistance** to families with dependent children, [click here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmydhrbenefits.dhr.state.md.us%2fdashboardClient%2f%23%2fhome&redir_log=540010106459415).

* For **state small business help** (separate from the Federal programs listed above), [click here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fcommerce.maryland.gov%2ffund%2fmaryland-small-business-covid-19-emergency-relief-fund-programs&redir_log=548180129753687).

|
---
[Please click here to unsubscribe](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//EsurveyForm.aspx?__cid=SenVanHollen&__sid=100007&__crop=15407.16260280.4540656.7517223)

I encourage anyone who needs this assistance to apply quickly. Emergency Economic InjuryGrants : We secured an additional $10 billion for these grants in the EIDL program. You can get the $10,000 grant by applying for an EIDL Loan. It does not have to be repaid, even if you do not meet the conditions to ultimately receive an EIDL loan. Work sharing: Maryland has an innovative work sharing program, and we secured federal funding to cover the full cost of expanding it. I encourage employers facing layoffs to consider this program. Find . Remaining challenges: Despite the progress made in Phase 3.5, we still need to fix problems that are preventing some small businesses, including many restaurants, from making effective use of PPP funds. For example, the 60-day forgivable loan period is not aligned with the time many businesses can expect to see customers return, and the Treasury Department rules put unreasonable limits on the share of PPP funds that can be used for fixed expenses like rent. I will continue fighting to improve these critical programs. For more information: See my for information on these programs and others. Unemployment insurance (UI) : I share the frustration of the many Marylanders who are having difficulty accessing unemployment benefits and the state's new application website, which launched Friday. My team and I have been in touch with the Maryland Department of Labor repeatedly, and we will keep monitoring and offering all possible assistance until these issues are fixed. Congress previously expanded UI to more workers, increased the amount of unemployment payments workers can receive by $600 per week, extended benefits by 13 weeks, and provided funds to help states process applications and get checks out faster. Your benefits, including the additional $600/week, will be backdated and paid based on your original eligibility date , so you will ultimately receive the help you urgently need. However, the backlog and delays are causing severe cash flow problems for many households. Unemployment insurance applicants, please note: If you are newly eligible for unemployment benefits in Maryland, such as independent contractors, I encourage you to apply at the State of Maryland's new unemployment website, . Select "Apply for Unemployment Insurance Benefits" in the left hand column. This will take you to the BEACON one-stop application page. The State's new, one-stop unemployment insurance application is designed to allow Marylanders to file claims for all unemployment programs entirely online. This includes the Pandemic Unemployment Assistance program, the Pandemic Emergency Unemployment Compensation program, and those who were previously required to file by phone like federal employees, members of the military, individuals who have worked in multiple states, and those who have worked for more than 3 employers in the last 18 months. If you have questions for the unemployment office, try reviewing before calling, because the phone hold times are very long. We continue to prioritize stopping the spread of the virus and ensuring that frontline healthcare providers are equipped to respond. The most recent legislation (Phase 3.5) adds an additional $75 billion to the $100 billion fund for hospitals, community health centers, senior care facilities and other health care providers. Importantly, this legislation also includes $25 billion to help build out a rapid national COVID-19 testing capability. Scientists and public health leaders agree: we can only get the virus under control, manage future outbreaks, and safely reopen our economy with a robust national strategy that expands access to COVID-19 testing. The next bill must build on this down payment to dramatically expand the national production and distribution of all the elements needed for a rapid, national testing capability, including a sufficient supply of swabs, transport kits, and reagents. While the additional relief in Phase 3.5 is urgently needed, the bill left out several important priorities. Many families need increased food aid, but Republicans in the Senate blocked additional relief. In addition, our state and local governments, and the emergency responders on the front lines of the battle against COVID-19, are experiencing severe revenue losses and need more help. While the CARES Act provided an important $150 billion down payment to support state and local governments, more is needed. And there are counterproductive restrictions on the use of the funds. I was appalled to hear Senator McConnell recklessly suggest that states and municipalities could just declare bankruptcy - we should be helping them support first responders, firefighters, and the many daily services our local governments provide. Providing additional and more flexible assistance to state and local governments must be a priority in the next relief bill. We need to end the digital divide that further deepens inequality in our schools. Too many of our students haven't had access to the internet at home to do homework -- and now to do their daily schoolwork. I was pleased to help lead the effort in the CARES Act to ensure that emergency funds for our schools can be used to provide students with internet access and devices. But the next round of legislation needs a larger pool of funds dedicated exclusively to building out high-speed internet access to every household. Like electricity in the 20th century, we should have universal access to high speed internet in the twenty-first. Americans overwhelmingly want our country to come together to solve these problems. We need to start work on the next major coronavirus relief bill immediately. There is so much more to do to ensure that the recovery, when it comes, lifts everyone - especially those too often left behind in the past. There are tough days ahead, but I believe the determination and generosity of spirit of our fellow Marylanders will see us through this crisis to better days. Chris Van Hollen To learn more about Senator Van Hollen's work to address the COVID-19 crisis, visit vanhollen.senate.gov/coronavirus. During the coronavirus crisis, many federal funds have been distributed to Maryland based on our population. It's a reminder of the importance of getting yourself counted! If you have not already done so, please head to to fill out the questionnaire right away, or do it by phone or mail. It takes less than ten minutes for most people. Protections and Resources for Marylanders In case you missed our last newsletter, here are some helpful resources. And you can always reach out to us if you need help with federal agencies or programs. You may call my office at 301-545-1500 or email mailto:assistance@vanhollen.senate.gov. No evictions: The state has ordered that no Maryland family or business who suffered a substantial loss of income due to COVID-19 or related closures can be evicted during the coronavirus state of emergency. The obligation to make rent and mortgage payments remains. Landlords may continue to file failure to pay rent cases, which will be considered after the state of emergency ends. No repossessions: Marylanders' cars, mobile homes, trailers, and house boats cannot be repossessed during the coronavirus emergency. The obligation to make rent and mortgage payments remains. Landlords may continue to file failure to pay rent cases, which will be considered after the state of emergency ends. Senior citizens seeking information and resources on COVID-19 can . To find school meals nearby, visit the Maryland State Department of Education's . To find child care , call Maryland's LOCATE referral system at 1-877-261-0060, or . For help paying utility bills , visit . For temporary cash assistance to families with dependent children, . For state small business help** (separate from the Federal programs listed above), . |

name: Congressman Al Green
subject: Texas needs more COVID-19 testing
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Keep Al Green in Congress
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/_mUcexpFtCGkUgPwWBfI_tyoAoURlzKR2QtPb6zYETo=/1y0zJi6n_nPtzWktkcJ-ZnNwx0N1vKUfKKo96jl4UII=)
---
| Folks, **This week, Congresswoman Sylvia Garcia and I penned two congressional letters urging Governor Abbott and the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to expand mobile COVID-19 testing sites for hard-hit communities here in Texas.** One of the most important steps in defeating COVID-19 and getting our country back to normal is testing. Without adequate testing, COVID-19 experts won't be able to understand the extent of the pandemic, which means they can't work effectively to stop it from spreading. And with Texas Governor Abbott planning on reopening large numbers of unessential businesses next week, the need for testing is more critical than ever. That's why Congresswoman Garcia and I are choosing to stand up for communities of color in Texas. It is well documented that people of color are dying at disproportionately high rates due to COVID-19. **As lawmakers, we have a responsibility to take care of our most vulnerable -- our Black, Latino, elderly, and other vulnerable communities.** We cannot leave these groups behind -- and expanded testing is critical to preventing further spread in these already struggling communities. And of course, increased testing will benefit all by reducing the spread of COVID-19, saving lives, and getting America back to work as soon and safely as possible. I and my colleagues in the House are continuing to do all we can to battle this pandemic and provide relief for struggling Americans. In the meantime, remember to continue social distancing and stay safe! In service and solidarity, Congressman Al Green
---
* * *
| | Congressman Al Green has served the people of Texas' 9th district for 15 years. He is a progressive leader in Washington and was the first Member of Congress to introduce articles of impeachment against President Trump on the House floor.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because we want to keep you fully informed about Al's work for justice, accountability, and progress. If you'd prefer not to hear from us, you can [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/_mUcexpFtCGkUgPwWBfI_tyoAoURlzKR2QtPb6zYETo=/jB6C5Ide6u-DDQvASrCNkYMhqQZmJzyYIUtpYn13oGQ=) here.

| Paid for and Authorized by Keep Al Green in Congress
---
Keep Al Green in Congress
P.O. Box 56761
Houston TX 77256-6761 United States

| ,

You may or may not know that my Republican challenger, Liberal, Lying Lisa Scheller, has used her big-money donors to create a website with gutter style, fabricated personal attacks on me.

Lisa Scheller has gone to desperate measures, calling in back up from the Washington Swamp itself.

**She handsomely paid Washington Insiders to smear me and my campaign in a desperate attempt to cover up her own agenda.**

Truthfully, this does not surprise me.

I am actually honored that she feels so threatened by my campaign that she would dump tons of money into a ridiculous and childish website.

_**THIS SHOWS THAT WE ARE WINNING THIS ELECTION!!**_

In response to Liberal, Lying Lisa Scheller’s feeble attempt to derail my campaign, I am launching a series: _**The Chronicles of Liberal, Lying Lisa Scheller**_.

Over the course of the next two weeks, I will expose her liberal agenda, her hypocrisy, and her _**exploitation of public office to make money for herself, and her company that received millions of dollars from the Obama Administration and from China.**_

, we have Liberal Lying Lisa Scheller on her heels, and I need your help to win this Primary Election!

**Will you help support the cause and chip in $50, $25, or even just $10 to my campaign?**

name: Mike
subject: LifeZette: RINO House candidate in New Mexico uses Trump as piñata
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Claire Chase for Congress, Inc
body:
---
| | | | | | Hey there,
Maybe this is why Yvette Herrell is spending so much money lying about Claire Chase.
LifeZette - the conservative news publication founded by Laura Ingraham - is out with an [investigative story](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-ZS2eia3KWm22QmRMn7U0N1Erqv05JExnaZ6h8U4YiC1enPBbr2i4_kPNlxWyZRxuzCngKScz0FA_WyqAZOU_L2vLaweX3E0RxQ8_-NNjG_4ptjjtSAhnEVm0Pp9-nXPP9Ey10B8zWxQYZeg-8kyyLVfld0HbFcTx7zKQwypNEg1LT_MKDoL-6AknJh3llZ1f7GuUV4T4fu38lcdiJ-jbJY22PLKW2qDSgZCSYqnj9HLOEzfr9BISQ==&c=I63H3ep-dStbD2wCnuiIOLklohRDJDzlJ4OfeAX91Tr0OamDO6qEdg==&ch=tCl4geivx4s2WSgeniLbo7dh-PEy-6oFPJyVEAf2zC5REypDIpyvwA==) on Yvette Herrell's claim that she supports President Trump. What they found isn't pretty. As a state legislator, Yvette billed New Mexico taxpayers to fly to a Never Trump piñata party in California where they hung the soon to be President in effigy.
New Mexicans deserve a refund and better representation.
---
| RINO House candidate in New Mexico uses Trump as piñata LifeZette 4/27/20 [https://www.lifezette.com/2020/04/rino-house-candidate-in-new-mexico-uses-trump-as-pinata/](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-ZS2eia3KWm22QmRMn7U0N1Erqv05JExnaZ6h8U4YiC1enPBbr2i4_kPNlxWyZRxuzCngKScz0FA_WyqAZOU_L2vLaweX3E0RxQ8_-NNjG_4ptjjtSAhnEVm0Pp9-nXPP9Ey10B8zWxQYZeg-8kyyLVfld0HbFcTx7zKQwypNEg1LT_MKDoL-6AknJh3llZ1f7GuUV4T4fu38lcdiJ-jbJY22PLKW2qDSgZCSYqnj9HLOEzfr9BISQ==&c=I63H3ep-dStbD2wCnuiIOLklohRDJDzlJ4OfeAX91Tr0OamDO6qEdg==&ch=tCl4geivx4s2WSgeniLbo7dh-PEy-6oFPJyVEAf2zC5REypDIpyvwA==)
There's a RINO running in the GOP primary in the New Mexico 2nd congressional district. Her name is Yvette Herrell and she is playing fast and loose with the truth when it comes to her support of President Trump. She claims to strongly support him. The facts say otherwise.
In 2015, after Trump entered the race, State Rep. Herrell used thousands in taxpayer money to fly out of state to a conference where the image of the soon-to-be GOP president was used in a pinata. Or they hung him in effigy, take your pick.
The Times of San Diego put it this way, "Donald Trump isn't at the 42nd annual meeting of ALEC in downtown San Diego -- unless you consider the piñata hanging at an exhibition hall booth, the real-estate magnate's name being invoked in private and a telling question by a famed Republican pollster. Frank Luntz, the pollster and 'maestro of messaging,' asked Thursday's huge luncheon crowd at the Manchester Grand Hyatt: 'How many of you are supporting Donald Trump?'
"Crickets. None of the hundreds of American Legislative Exchange Council members or guests, including more than 400 lawmakers, said a word." And Herrell, using taxpayer funds, was right there.
Okay, so that was before Trump was president and he was in a contested primary with other worthy Republicans. But this is from last month, March 11, 2020, in the middle of the coronavirus crisis.
According to the Associated Press, "A Republican U.S. House candidate in a crucial New Mexico race said Monday the GOP-controlled Congress and the Trump Administration showed a 'lack of leadership' during President Donald Trump's first two years in office.
"Speaking to podcast host Daniel Horowitz, former state lawmaker Yvette Herrell said she felt GOP leaders in the House and Senate could have done more... She also faulted the Trump administration for failing to tackle issues that 'could have been put to bed.'"
Combine this with the fact that she lost to a hard-left Democrat in the 2018 general election, in a district the president won by 10 points in 2016, and it doesn't add up to flipping the district red. Comments Mike Berg, campaign manager for Herrell's conservative GOP primary opponent Claire Chase: "Yvette Herrell has repeatedly lied about her support for President Trump. The fact that she used our tax dollars to fly to a swanky California conference where they hung the President in effigy disqualifies her as a Republican candidate."
---
|
---
| ###
---
| [ClaireChase.org](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-ZS2eia3KWm22QmRMn7U0N1Erqv05JExnaZ6h8U4YiC1enPBbr2i4xyLvowsQPRagCLuXas796iYVx1NIybEMm2j704ivN5AdD_Pfi9RSKwdn-jgCds9mOYMXoyB8sbCUOru5t7jdrrq1PzCJupshQ==&c=I63H3ep-dStbD2wCnuiIOLklohRDJDzlJ4OfeAX91Tr0OamDO6qEdg==&ch=tCl4geivx4s2WSgeniLbo7dh-PEy-6oFPJyVEAf2zC5REypDIpyvwA==)

subject: POLL: MN approves of state's handling of COVID-19
disclaimer_text: paid for by the DFL House Caucus
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/Zd26FJiI3Q5i8k4OaoEX4U3GzFEF5ZBGWMZ6CABJLHM=/1xcjx6bM17XORxoCS5Wcu-nhw6CxYPO9xDZZ1OJn_Ww=?refcode=042720)
| | Friend, Today a poll was made public that shows Minnesotans overwhelmingly approve of Governor Walz and the House DFL's [handling of the COVID-19 pandemic](https://act.myngp.com/el/Zd26FJiI3Q5i8k4OaoEX4U3GzFEF5ZBGWMZ6CABJLHM=/JJONmwHf1ekdEiGD0RkfEKV7kT2dsjq9F31d8HsmXfw=).
* * *
**_How would you assess the state of Minnesota 's response to the virus: _** **_73%_** _think its response has been about right _ **_22%_**_think it 's over reacting _ _5% think it 's under reacting _ **_Do you approve or disapprove of the job Governor Tim Walz is doing handling the coronavirus?_** **_74%_**_Approve_ **_18%_** _Disapprove_ _9% Not Sure_
* * *
Time and time again, **we 've seen Republicans deny the severity of the pandemic that could put Minnesotans' health and lives at risk**, House DFLers are continuing to work on solutions to ensure Minnesotans can make ends meet during the pandemic and beyond. [**If you can afford it, we would really appreciate a donation to help us expand and defend our majority this year and continue to pass legislation that helps all Minnesotans recover from this difficult time.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/Zd26FJiI3Q5i8k4OaoEX4U3GzFEF5ZBGWMZ6CABJLHM=/1xcjx6bM17XORxoCS5Wcu-nhw6CxYPO9xDZZ1OJn_Ww=?refcode=042720) Minnesotans care about one another, and we help each other out in tough times. Despite the challenges and uncertainty before us, we remain confident in our ability to come together and pull through the COVID-19 public health crisis. Thank you, House DFL Team [P.S. In Minnesota you can donate $50 (or $100 per couple) and it will be **completely refunded** to you by the state through the Minnesota Political Contribution Refund program.](https://act.myngp.com/el/Zd26FJiI3Q5i8k4OaoEX4U3GzFEF5ZBGWMZ6CABJLHM=/1xcjx6bM17XORxoCS5Wcu-nhw6CxYPO9xDZZ1OJn_Ww=?refcode=042720) [**Donate**](https://act.myngp.com/el/Zd26FJiI3Q5i8k4OaoEX4U3GzFEF5ZBGWMZ6CABJLHM=/1xcjx6bM17XORxoCS5Wcu-nhw6CxYPO9xDZZ1OJn_Ww=?refcode=042720)

Millions of Americans are staying at home, and reaching them has never been more important in an election campaign.

From the start of this race, I’ve talked about making this a 159-county campaign to reach Georgians from every walk of life. **That hasn’t changed — but we’ve had to adjust our tactics to keep everyone safe.**

Starting tomorrow, our first ad “Invisible” will be running on television. This is a MAJOR investment for our campaign, friend. One of my opponents is already on the air, and with just three days to go before our April deadline, I need you to help keep our ad running.

[**Since I don’t take corporate PAC money, this new ad is financed by your individual contributions. We’ve set a goal of raising $25,000 online before Thursday — can I count on you to donate right now so our first major television ad can reach households all throughout Georgia?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17286852/189334543/777589997?refcode=em042720-pm&refcode2=2e217317-e588-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZTIxNzMxNy1lNTg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TTydssb8-_dW8q7CTRJ6AINn5m2ug2WV9VLhLZ_s3TM=) | | If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:

The _Atlanta Journal-Constitution_ ’s polling says that this race is wide open, and the national media is starting to catch on that Georgia is the key to the Senate majority. **Republicans know this, which is why Donald Trump made David Perdue the first senator he went to for personal fundraising assistance.**

Vice President Mike Pence, Mitch McConnell’s Senate PAC, and millions of dollars in contributions from corporate PACs are also propping up Perdue, and **despite Perdue’s campaign finance violations that are under FEC investigation, it may not matter if we don’t defeat him.**

[**If each of you rushed in as little as $5, we could fully fund this ad through the next week. Let’s show Perdue, McConnell, and Trump the consequences of invisible leadership in a crisis.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17286861/189334552/777589997?refcode=em042720-pm&refcode2=2e217317-e588-ea11-86e9-00155d03b5dd&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZTIxNzMxNy1lNTg4LWVhMTEtODZlOS0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TTydssb8-_dW8q7CTRJ6AINn5m2ug2WV9VLhLZ_s3TM=)

clean_body: | | | | = ** | | Friend, Millions of Americans are staying at home, and reaching them has never been more important in an election campaign. From the start of this race, I’ve talked about making this a 159-county campaign to reach Georgians from every walk of life. That hasn’t changed — but we’ve had to adjust our tactics to keep everyone safe. Starting tomorrow, our first ad “Invisible” will be running on television. This is a MAJOR investment for our campaign, friend. One of my opponents is already on the air, and with just three days to go before our April deadline, I need you to help keep our ad running. = | | If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: = = = = The Atlanta Journal-Constitution ’s polling says that this race is wide open, and the national media is starting to catch on that Georgia is the key to the Senate majority. Republicans know this, which is why Donald Trump made David Perdue the first senator he went to for personal fundraising assistance. Vice President Mike Pence, Mitch McConnell’s Senate PAC, and millions of dollars in contributions from corporate PACs are also propping up Perdue, and despite Perdue’s campaign finance violations that are under FEC investigation, it may not matter if we don’t defeat him. = We cannot wait — our deadline is just days away. Thank you for being in this fight with me. Teresa PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE PMB 263, 3201 Macon Rd Ste 139, Columbus, GA 31906 =

You’ll want to read this — **__FIRST:__** Major election forecasters rated our race as the Democrats’ BEST chance to flip a Senate seat come November. We’re ahead in the polls and John’s Republican opponent is one of the most vulnerable Senators up for re-election. **__THEN:__** Mitch McConnell and his shady donor network dumped huge sums of money into key battleground states across the country — including Colorado where they plan to launch nasty attacks aimed at tearing John down. **__NOW:__** _The Washington Post_ has ranked us as a **“toss-up”** just as the right-wing Koch Network announced a huge on-the-ground effort to defeat us. **__SO:__** It’s never been more important for us to fight back against the GOP’s massive donor network. name: emergencypetition@protectvoting.org
subject: We can stop Trump's DAILY LIES!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Voter Protection Project and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
---
| |

**EMERGENCY PETITION: We MUST pressure news networks to STOP live broadcasting Trump 's daily lies.**

**[Add your name now->](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04d3/72412298/4fbb949d/61048344/659214604/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "http://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200427_LiveTrump_U1_X1?&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+now+rarr&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1")**
* * *
There's no mistaking it, Peter: When Donald Trump takes to the podium at White House for his daily coronavirus press conferences, he's LYING to the American public -- and costing more lives in the process. **That 's why we're DEMANDING major news networks REFUSE to air his press conferences live. But we need 1O,OOO Democrats to put the pressure on -- [so please add your name right away.](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04d3/72412298/4fbb949d/61048347/659214604/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "http://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200427_LiveTrump_U1_X1?&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2+-+so+please+add+your+name+right+away&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1")** We're not the only ones who know Trump's lies are dangerous: | [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04d3/72412298/4fbb949d/61048346/659214604/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200427_LiveTrump_U1_X1?&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1")
---
Let's be honest: Donald Trump is an international disgrace. But we know he won't ever change his narcissistic tendencies, so we have to go another route.
**That 's why we are mounting pressure on major news networks to STOP airing Trump's daily hour-long stream of lies at his press conferences.**
_After all, it 's their journalistic duty to report FACTS!_
[](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04d3/72412298/4fbb949d/61048349/659214604/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200427_LiveTrump_U1_X1?&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1")
---
Thanks for taking a stand with us,
_\-- the Voter Protection Project _

| [Voter Protection Project](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04d3/72412298/4fbb949d/61048348/659214604/VEsD/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protectvotingorg?refcode=protectvotingorg_headerlogo&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=5+-+Voter+Protection+Project&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1")
---

---

We believe that emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, you can [click here.](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04d3/72412298/4fbb949d/6104834a/659214604/VEsB/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://voterprotect.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/less-emails?utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=7+-+click+here&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1")
If you'd like to unsubscribe from all our emails, [click here.](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04d3/72412298/4fbb949d/6104834d/659214604/VEsO/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://voterprotect.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=8+-+click+here&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200427_TrumpLies_U1_X1")

body:
We don't need another rubber stamp in the Senate.
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa165/18d6a294/4cbf4ae1/26d61751/3857465860/VEsH/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | Team -- President Trump mishandled and delayed our country's response to the current health crisis, downplaying its seriousness and compounding the health care issues Tennesseans are already facing like rampant rural hospital closures and the opioid epidemic. That hasn't stopped Sen. Marsha Blackburn from praising the president's response to the crisis. **Blackburn has always been an unwavering rubber stamp for Trump, praising his policies even when they hurt hardworking Tennesseans. And our opponent promises to be her clone.** We can't afford another rubber stamp for Trump in the Senate. [_**James isn 't taking a dime of corporate PAC money, so we're counting on strong grassroots support to stop another Blackburn from getting elected. body:

Peter,
Tomorrow is the biggest day yet for this campaign. After Governor Mike DeWine postponed last month's primary election, a new date was set for a vote by mail primary election for tomorrow, April 28th.
**A strong showing in tomorrow 's election is critical for the future of this campaign.** If we don't create a big lead over Mike Turner's opponent, it will spell disaster for November.
That's why we're planning one final push to surge support ahead of tomorrow's election. If we can create the needed momentum in Mike's favor today, we will be able to hold the line tomorrow against Pelosi and Mike's Washington lobbyist opponent.
[_**Will you help us ensure we have the needed resources to make one final push ahead of tomorrow 's election? **_](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=24&ms=NDIzNjgzODYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0Mjg1MjQ4MAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
[_**Peter, we can 't let Mike's opponent get a lead on us after tomorrow!**_](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=NDIzNjgzODYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0Mjg1MjQ4MAS2&mt=1&rt=0) As a Washington lobbyist and Nancy Pelosi ally, she would ignore the needs of the Miami Valley and Southwest Ohio if elected. This would place the future of our communities in jeopardy. [_**It has never been more critical for grassroots supporters like you to step up and help us build momentum in Mike 's favor! **_](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=NDIzNjgzODYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTc0Mjg1MjQ4MAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
